clusters.cluster_interpretation: This cluster comprises harnesses optimized for offline evaluation of local models through batch inference, with strong provisioning and assessment capabilities but minimal support for interactive agents, remote APIs, synthetic data generation, or advanced reporting—serving primarily code, retrieval, and performance-focused benchmarking domains where reproducibility and self-contained execution are prioritized over real-time interaction or complex visualization.

These harnesses specialize in evaluating sequential decision-making agents (RL, multi-agent, robotics) through interactive simulation environments with deterministic scoring, but lack support for API-based models, LLM judges, and production-scale evaluation workflows, positioning them as domain-specific tools for controlled agent benchmarking rather than general-purpose LLM or retrieval evaluation.

This cluster comprises harnesses specialized in evaluating systems against pre-computed ground truth on static datasets, supporting standard installation and deterministic/subjective scoring but lacking support for interactive agents, remote model APIs, and advanced reporting—positioning them as lightweight, self-contained evaluation tools for academic benchmarks and offline metrics computation.

These harnesses excel at static benchmark evaluation with deterministic and latent metrics (Stage 3: 0.667), but lack interactive/stateful execution (no S2-A3), LLM judges (no S1-C2), and advanced reporting features (no S4-A5/A6), positioning them for offline comparison of algorithms and embeddings on fixed datasets rather than dynamic agent or production evaluation.

This cluster specializes in evaluating language models and retrieval systems through subjective LLM-as-judge assessment with dashboard reporting, unified by mandatory support for remote model APIs, judge preparation, and interactive result visualization—but notably lacking support for interactive agents, simulation environments, and deterministic metrics that would serve robotics or traditional ML evaluation.

This cluster comprises harnesses specialized in offline LLM evaluation using predefined benchmarks and ground truth, excelling at scoring and aggregation (Stage 3) but lacking support for interactive agents, synthetic data generation, production traffic, and advanced reporting—positioning them as standardized evaluation platforms for comparing model capabilities on static test sets rather than dynamic or real-world scenarios.
